page: Microsoft Azure D16ps_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DPSv5 server family. It is powered by an Ampere Neoverse-N1 ARMv8 processor operating at 3.0 GHz. The instance features 16 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, providing 16 vCPUs. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, translating to 4.0 GB of memory per core. The CPU cache configuration includes 64 KB of L1 data cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. This server does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, though it is Premium Storage capable.

Performance benchmarks place the D16ps_v5 in the average tier for most multi-core and memory operations. The instance achieved a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 28,878.29 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 9,616.0. Memory bandwidth is robust, reaching 1127.64 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 133.94 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 1,119.0 and a weak performance in Passmark CPU encryption tests. LLM inference speeds are also limited, with prompt processing for a 7B model registering at 19.03 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture offers a balanced resource density of 4 GB of RAM per vCPU, making it a viable option for scale-out workloads that benefit from dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading. The server is well-suited for applications such as static web serving, where it demonstrates a throughput of 18.71 GB/sec, as well as database operations, software compilation, and general-purpose containerized applications.
description: Microsoft Azure D16ps_v5 is an ARM64-based general-purpose virtual machine from the DPSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Ampere Neoverse-N1 ARMv8 processor with 16 dedicated physical cores, operating at 3.0 GHz, and 64.0 GB of RAM. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks indicate average multi-core performance, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 9,616.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 28,878.29, alongside a static web serving throughput of 18.71 GB/sec. However, single-core and cryptographic performance are relatively weak. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for scale-out workloads, making it suitable for web serving, database operations, software compilation, and general-purpose application hosting.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16ps_v5 is an ARM64 general-purpose virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 cores, 64 GB of RAM, and average multi-core performance for web serving and database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16ps_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine with 16 dedicated cores and 64 GB RAM, optimized for general-purpose and web serving workloads.
tagline: An ARM64-powered virtual machine featuring sixteen dedicated physical cores and balanced memory for efficient scale-out workloads and web serving.
bullet_points: Powered by a 16-core Ampere Neoverse-N1 ARMv8 processor at 3.0 GHz
Equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio
Delivers 18.71 GB/sec throughput for static web serving
Features dedicated physical cores with no hyperthreading for predictable execution
Best suited for web serving, database operations, and software compilation
